description: From the highest (at 1,000 feet) of the Kunde's vineyards, it's lavishly oaked, with aromas of butterscotch, spice and custard; also nutty and creamy notes. Marked by intense tropical fruit aromas and oriental spices, it also tastes very rich. It's the best of a very good trio of limited-production Chardonnay wines.
title: Kunde 1998 C.S. Ridge Vineyard Chardonnay (California)

description: This massively oaky wine smells like an explosion in a spice factory, with ginger, cinnamon, clove and nutmeg aromas, accented by vanilla, brown sugar, butterscotch and honey. Somewhere in there is some ripe tropical fruit. Enormous and full-bodied, it's a dense, unctuous wine.
title: Signorello 1998 Hope's Cuvée Chardonnay (California)

description: A solid effort from this fine winery, from a vintage everyone is increasingly admitting was more difficult than it looked. It’s as middle of the road as you can get. It strikes all the right notes in terms of ripe, polished black-currant fruit, anise and smoky oak, yet never quite achieves complexity. Nonetheless there’s enough harmony and juiciness to satisfy.
title: Merryvale 1998 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: A medium to deep ruby color opens things up. The wine is well-balanced and the tannins soft. Unfortunately, however, this structure is fleshed out with only light coffee notes, and therein lies the problem.
title: Backsberg 1998 Merlot (Paarl)

description: Simple fruit and a sweet, almost candied flavor. This wine seems unbalanced. There's an odd hole in the middle, and very little weight or tannin in the finish. It's all forward, sweet fruit, more like candy than berries.
title: Buffalo Ridge 1998 French Camp Zinfandel (Central Coast)

description: Extracted, tannic, dark and dense, with briary, jammy fruit and thick tannins. Blackberries and chocolate, set off with lots of acid. It's a sensational, balanced, bold, ageworthy Zin.
title: St. Francis 1998 Pagani Reserve Zinfandel (Sonoma Valley)

description: Attractive blackberry aromas with bright tobacco and herb accents open this well-balanced wine. The palate shows strong cassis, earth and herb flavors, and an even mouthfeel. Finishes with dark cherry notes and full, fine tannins. This will age nicely and can use a little time. Quite flavorful and elegant.
title: Penfolds 1998 Bin 407 Cabernet Sauvignon (South Australia)

description: This is one of the most interesting Merlots of the vintage because it combines fruity ripeness with herbal and vegetal notes to create a complex wine. Blackberry and cassis, a bit of stewed tomato and mushroom, grilled meat, green olives and roasted coffee beans only begin to describe it. Dry and harmonious.
title: Ledson 1998 Merlot (Sonoma Valley)

description: A négociant bottling from the ubiquitous Yvon Mau, this wine proudly boasts élevé en futs du chene on the label. You can certainly notice it in the cedary aromas and flavors, but it's adequately balanced by cassis and tobacco flavors. The fruit tastes a touch overripe, but it seems to work well here.
title: Premius 1998  Bordeaux

description: Firm, tannic and fruity, with some nice tropical fruit flavors, wrapped around stiff tannins and some green, slightly stemmy flavors. The finish is hard and rough.
title: Castle 1998 Zinfandel (Russian River Valley)

description: Alcoholic, volatile nose, with some pickley edges to it. The wine is smooth and fruity, with a quick, hot, alcoholic finish and very dry tannins.
title: Sobon Estate 1998 Zinfandel (Fiddletown)

description: An atypically oaky wine for Siduri, with a rich veneer of French oak overlying black cherries, cola and spice. The intensity builds on the palate, where root beer and black cherries culminate in a long, smoky-spicy finish. Needs a couple of years to pull itself together, but the raw materials are all there.
title: Siduri 1998 Archery Summit Vineyard Pinot Noir (California)

description: Commanding nose, with cherry, purple basil, mineral and meaty elements. Fine depth of fruit, with sweet-tart and earthy notes, and a brisk mouthfeel. The tangy finish with its tart cherry, mineral and earth complexity holds you a long time. Serious, and fulfills Domaine Drouhin Oregon's promise to make great Pinot Noir on a par with Burgundy.
title: Domaine Drouhin 1998 Pinot Noir (Oregon)

description: An ultrapure nose and tons of polish and precision define this tasty reserve-style blend of several vineyards. Pretty much an easy-drinker, with an undercurrent of sweet cherries and vanilla. It hits all chords and fades away smooth. Style and power come in proper proportions. Will age nicely for at least five years.
title: Panther Creek 1998 Reserve Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Broad and obvious in its appeal, with peach marmalade and applesauce tastes and saccharine sweetness. There also seems to be a smoky vanilla streak from some kind of oak. It's a country-style common wine and there's not much going on beyond the fruit.
title: King Estate 1998 Chardonnay (Oregon)

description: Sure it's oaky, but it tastes good, with the subtle menthol and bold vanilla flavors serving to highlight bright cherries. The silky texture is a highlight, then the finish shows off juicy fruit and fine tannins.
title: Le Rouge de Saint-Louis 1998 Red (Côtes de Provence)

description: Dark cherry, toasted oak and licorice notes mark the nose of this fairly full wine. Sweet and sour flavors, a smoky note, and an even finish with tarry elements all have significant appeal. The toasted-oak notes seem a bit heavy handed, but tasty.
title: Longridge 1998 Pinotage (Stellenbosch)

description: A solid effort for North Fork Cabernet, which has a tendency towards inconsistency. This has dark cassis fruit underlined by a weedy, herbal streak and a dried-fruit nuance as well.
title: Gristina 1998 Cabernet Sauvignon (North Fork of Long Island)

description: This excellent and rich wine smells beautiful, and coats the mouth with velvety smoothness. The flavors range from green olives to tobacco and blackberries, with oaky complexities. The finish is dry, spicy and intense.
title: Gallo of Sonoma 1998 Frei Ranch Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Dry Creek Valley)

description: Rather dilute, it shows slight black-cherry and blackberry aromas and flavors. The overall impression is really little more than alcohol and faint body. Finishes very dry, with innocuous tannins.
title: Alfasi 1998 Reserve Merlot (Maule Valley)

description: Tasty and smooth, this Rioja shows sweet black cherry fruit and licorice aromas and flavors. There's plenty of oak here, too, plus some tannins to lose on the even, dry menthol and leather-tinged close. Will benefit from a year or two in bottle, and may shine quite nicely if the fruit comes forward a bit more. Drink now–2007. Best Buy. —M.M.
title: Martinez Bujanda 1998 Conde de Valdemar Crianza 1998  (Rioja)

description: Marked by powerful oak influences and sur-lie aging, this Kunde offering features strong, forward aromas of the ripest pears and apples, but maintains a light quality on the palate.
title: Kunde 1998 Wildwood Vineyard Chardonnay (California)

description: Always a bit herbaceous, this year the underlying leanness shows in rather green fruit, veering into bell pepper, dill, mint and oregano. All the oak in France and America can't really make the fruit any riper. But the oak does add smoky, creamy, vanilla notes and sweetness, too. There is some pretty fruit—notably blackberry—in the finish, but still, this is a wine of structure. It's fantastically well made, a classy quaff, and it will pair well with rich red meats, especially beef.
title: Dry Creek Vineyard 1998 Meritage Red (Sonoma County)

description: Starts out with rough, earthy blackberry and cassis aromas, with deeper notes of plum and chocolate. Drinks very soft and velvety, almost too much so; seems to lack acidity and vibrancy. At this price, though, you can’t expect miracles.
title: Monterey Vineyard 1998 Cabernet Sauvignon (Monterey County)

description: Woody notes mix with leather and light brown sugar that carry to the palate with cedar, tobacco and meaty red plum. The oaking is well integrated, the meatiness is attractive and the wine is a great example of South Africa's Old World bent.
title: Môreson 1998 Cabernet Sauvignon (Coastal Region)

description: This nuovo mondo Super Tuscan represents the first vintage of Borgo Scopeto under the management of Tenuta Caparzo, and it has a tremendous jammy, extracted quality to it, from the smooth, deep plum and blackberry nose to the soft, rich palate that exudes tons of black cherry fruit along with coffee and licorice. It's a mix of 60% Sangiovese, 20% Cabernet Sauvignon and 20% Syrah, and everything is working in unison.
title: Borgo Scopeto 1998 Borgonero Sangiovese (Toscana)

description: A very nice wine, with good, ripe black-currant fruit; dry and balancedwith aromas and flavors of sweet, smoky oak. The flash is on the surface and it's a little sharp, so it's not an ager. But it's a tasty wine.
title: Beringer 1998 Cabernet Sauvignon (Knights Valley)

description: Very deep, lush and welcoming in the nose—there’s an unmistakable heavy color and look to it. It’s a ripe and ready wine that should pick up complexity with bottle age. Yes, there’s a one-dimensionality to it because it’s a single-clone wine, but you should really enjoy the cherry and plum sweetness at its core.
title: Bethel Heights 1998 Wadenswil Block Reserve Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: There’s a persistent menthol note running through this wine that may prove controversial. My money is that it will integrate with time (say, 2–3 years) into the otherwise rich cherry fruit. The finish is explosively fruity and long, which bodes well for the future.
title: Erath 1998 Reserve Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Deep cassis aromas, a touch of licorice, and menthol notes on the nose open to a palate of black-currant and cocoa flavors in this well-balanced, medium-weight wine. Nicely structured, it finishes with good length and moderate tannins; can be cellared a few years. Best Buy.
title: Jacob's Creek 1998 Cabernet Sauvignon (Barossa Valley)

description: This is a very distinctive, even eccentric, Cab that must reflect its single-vineyard terroir.  The usual black-currant and blackberry notes are there, but the aroma is sharply dominated by an herbal streak, somewhat stalky or green, as if some of the grapes didn't ripen.  Tastes a little sharp.  Purists might want to age it for 3-5 years to see if it softens up.
title: Errazuriz 1998 Don Maximiano Founder's Reserve Red (Aconcagua Valley)

description: With a bouquet of dark plum and toasty oak, with an earthy barnyardy note, this wine from this older Bordeaux grape still cultivated in Chile has medium weight and some plum and caramel flavors. In the end, the wood is a bit much for the fruit, and it is not dissimilar to some Barberas from Italy's Piedmont that wear an overweight of oak.
title: Undurraga 1998 Reserva Carmenère (Colchagua Valley)

description: This light to medium weight Cabernet has the right profile, but just doesn't seem to come alive. The nose shows blackberry and tobacco aromas and in the mouth there are dried black cherry and spice notes, but they never shine. A soflty dry, slightly woody finish closes the package.
title: Cousiño-Macul 1998 Cabernet Sauvignon (Maipo Valley)

description: Soft, plum and cherry fruit scents, augmented with hints of rose petal and cranberry. In the mouth it's lean and insubstantial, with a hard, tannic finish.
title: Mill Creek 1998 Zinfandel (Dry Creek Valley)

description: Delicious berries and cream nose, which follows through with crisp, spicy flavors and good structure. OVOC stands for “old vines old clones” and the fruit, from head-pruned, hillside vineyards, lives up to its billing. Great all-purpose Zin.
title: Alderbrook 1998 OVOC Zinfandel (Sonoma County)

description: Orange, apricot and pineapple aromas ride over a deep bass note of earth and spice. The earth nearly takes over on the palate, providing a rich, complex counterpoint to the fruit-driven sweetness. Could use a touch more acidity to really make the flavors sing.
title: Wagner 1998 Ice Wine Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: Scents of pineapple and hazelnuts open into a lush, spicy, forward, slightly toasty wine with immediate drinkablility and appeal.
title: Louis Latour 1998  Chassagne-Montrachet

description: Green-streaked barnyard aromas couple with black cherries and tobacco in this varietally true offering. Medium-weight on the palate, maybe even a bit low in acidity. Decent, but doesn’t truly sing.
title: High Pass 1998 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Very light in color, so much so that it’s almost transparent. Potent aromas of mushroom, bramble and buttery oak. Some strawberry and cream flavors are driven by snappy acids. There’s length and kick to the fruit-forward finish.
title: Benton-Lane 1998 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Defines Carneros in its rich, nuanced aromas of fig, ripe peach, tangerine, nutmeg and oaky vanilla. Winemaker-induced complexities from barrel fermentation and lees aging. Tremendous depth of flavor, it just sings on the palate and is so rich and spicy. The ultralong finish is sweet and satisfying.
title: Schug 1998 Chardonnay (Carneros)

description: This round, soft, alcoholic wine deserves praise for its spicy-peppery warmth and abundant cherry fruit. Those same qualities are also its biggest shortcoming: The low acidity and high alcohol make it seem a bit heavy. —J.C
title: Temple Bruer 1998 Cornucopia Grenache (Langhorne Creek)

description: Browning and maturing, but still showing a ton of life and kick. This traditional-style wine is mildly roasted on the nose, with touches of leather, latex and other firm aromas. The palate is nice, with dry raspberry and cherry flavors finishing with power and length. Solid and respectable, but not something you'd label as special. Drink now–2014.
title: Bodegas Faustino 1998 Faustino de Autor Reserva Especial  (Rioja)

description: A lot of wood, still pretty rough, surrounds a thick, tannic, muscular, ripe, rustic, Old World-style Zin. There's nothing elegant about this wine, but it packs a lot of flavor into a sturdy frame.
title: Lolonis 1998 Tollini Vineyard Zinfandel (Redwood Valley)

description: This wine is beginning to get some age to it, but remains fresh. There is some toast, even coffee, while the aftertaste is soft and slightly candied.
title: Duval-Leroy 1998 Blanc de Blancs Chardonnay (Champagne)
